requirement.original: When a user grants /allow permission for a command, the hook system still blocks execution. Specific observed case: the command was a Python interpreter located under /tmp (e.g., /tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python), running a script at /root/orchestra/docs/dev/find_red_cells.py, with stdout redirected to a file at /root/orchestra/docs/research/case-3. The /allow sentinel grant existed but execution was still blocked. Root cause is unknown — candidates are: (1) hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh has a daemon-restart-wrapper or similar pattern that matches /tmp/ interpreter paths and blocks regardless of any sentinel grant, (2) the stdout redirection is handled as a separate operation whose target is not covered by the grant op/target fields, (3) the hooks/lib/allowlist.py match logic does not correctly handle commands where the executable path is under /tmp. Investigate which hook actually blocked this command, trace through the allowlist matching logic to find the gap, and determine what precise change is needed so that an /allow grant covering this type of command (venv interpreter under /tmp, script path, output redirect) is correctly honored.
requirement.clarified: Three gaps in the /allow grant system prevent the command from being honored: (A) IS_SUBAGENT firewall blocks all grant paths in subagent context; (B) CF-1 suppresses legacy fallback when a regex/wildcard sentinel exists but cannot structurally match (op='*' never equals any real command token); (C) sentinel args_contain construction is redirect-unaware — the redirect token '> /output' lands in args_contain where it may not appear in the hook's view of the command.
requirement.what: Fix three gaps in the /allow grant system so that user-granted permissions for /tmp venv interpreter commands with output redirects are correctly honored
requirement.why: User needs to run Python scripts from venv interpreters installed under /tmp (e.g., xlsx-venv) and redirect output to files; the /allow grant mechanism fails silently due to structural bugs in sentinel construction and CF-1 gating logic

included: Sentinel construction in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh: redirect token stripping from args_contain; no-sentinel for regex/wildcard grants
CF-1 gate in pretool-bash-safety.sh: legacy_fallback_allowed exemption for sentinels that cannot structurally match
settings.json allow list: add /tmp/*-venv/bin/python* entry as secondary fix
excluded: Subagent write firewall in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh Step 0
Any change to the AC2 comment-only attack invariant
IS_SUBAGENT firewall for legacy pattern-string grants

success_criteria: User issues /allow /tmp/xlsx-venv/bin/python <script> and the command is allowed by pretool-bash-safety.sh (SENTINEL_OK or CONSUMED log)
Redirect tokens (> /path) in the /allow pattern are not stored in args_contain
Bare /allow or regex /allow grants do not trigger CF-1 suppression of legacy path
Daemon-restart and subagent firewalls remain fully intact
constraints: AC2 comment-only attack invariant must be preserved
IS_SUBAGENT firewall for legacy grants must remain unchanged
All Layer 1.A–1.E daemon-restart prohibition blocks must remain intact

root_cause_analysis.symptom: User issues /allow for a /tmp venv Python command with output redirect; command is still blocked
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Three independent gaps: (A) IS_SUBAGENT firewall unconditionally discards all grant paths in subagent contexts; (B) CF-1 at pretool-bash-safety.sh:517-527 sets SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1 on SENTINEL_EXISTS_NO_MATCH, which suppresses legacy path at line 551 — this fires for regex/wildcard grants because their op='*' never equals any real command's head_op; (C) sentinel construction in userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:196-205 puts the full remainder of the pattern (including redirect '> /output-path') into args_contain[0] as one string, which fails if the hook payload omits or normalizes the redirect
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: 28a1e85 feat(hooks): allowlist consolidation + push-sentinel + AC verify suite — CF-1 invariant was added here; sentinel construction present in this commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: CF-1 was added as a security invariant to prevent comment-only bypass attacks (e.g., 'echo hi # rm -rf /') against legacy substring matching. The sentinel args_contain construction was intended to capture the full allow pattern faithfully.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: CF-1 is too broad: it fires for regex/wildcard grants whose sentinel op='*' can never structurally match any real command, permanently suppressing legacy for all regex grants. The args_contain construction does not distinguish shell redirect tokens from actual command arguments.
observations: pretool-bash-safety.sh:145 check_and_consume_allowlist() returns 1 immediately when IS_SUBAGENT=1 (inferred: code reading)
pretool-bash-safety.sh:483 entire sentinel block wrapped in IS_SUBAGENT != 1 condition (inferred: code reading)
pretool-bash-safety.sh:517-527 SENTINEL_EXISTS_NO_MATCH branch sets SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1 (inferred: code reading)
pretool-bash-safety.sh:551 CF-1 gate: check_and_consume_allowlist called only when SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK != 1 (inferred: code reading)
userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:197-199 regex grants produce op='*' sentinel entry (inferred: code reading)
lib/allowlist.py:470-471 match requires want_op == head_op (exact string equality) — '*' never equals any real command token (inferred: code reading)
userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh:196-205 args_contain = [rest] where rest = everything after first whitespace-delimited token, including redirect '> /path' (inferred: code reading)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — no runtime logs available to confirm which specific path fired)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: No runtime logs available. Root cause localized by static code tracing through three independent code paths in pretool-bash-safety.sh and userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh. All three gaps are independently verifiable by reading the source at the cited lines.

measured.value: IS_SUBAGENT gate at line 145 of pretool-bash-safety.sh returns 1 unconditionally for subagents; CF-1 at line 517-527 sets SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1 on SENTINEL_EXISTS_NO_MATCH; regex grants produce op='*' sentinel which never matches head_op; args_contain includes redirect tokens from raw pattern split

expected.value: When user issues /allow for a specific command, that command should exit pretool-bash-safety.sh with exit 0 (allowed). The SENTINEL_OK branch at line 509-515 should fire, or the CONSUMED branch at line 963-998 should fire.
expected.source: CLAUDE.md Sentinel-grant mechanism description; hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh design intent as documented in comments lines 474-535

evidence.gap: Three gaps prevent the intended allow: (A) subagent context bypasses all paths; (B) regex/wildcard sentinels trigger CF-1 suppression of legacy; (C) redirect tokens in args_contain cause structural mismatch

reference_source.description: Direct code reading of hook source files in this session

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempts on this specific issue

context.codebase_state: modified settings.json and various docs/dev files pending commit
context.recent_commits: 28a1e85 feat(hooks): allowlist consolidation + push-sentinel + AC verify suite

development_approach.strategy: Three targeted fixes, each independently verifiable: (1) In userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh, strip redirect tokens from args_contain during sentinel construction; (2) In userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh, skip writing sentinel for regex/wildcard grants (is_regex=true); (3) In pretool-bash-safety.sh CF-1 block, add fallback option for sentinels with legacy_fallback_allowed field OR rely on fix (2) meaning regex grants never produce a sentinel. Additionally add Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*:*) to settings.json allow list.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA writes unit tests for: (a) sentinel construction with redirect pattern — verify args_contain excludes > token; (b) regex /allow — verify no sentinel written; (c) literal /allow for /tmp path — verify sentinel written with correct op and args_contain; (d) pretool-bash-safety.sh CF-1 path — simulate SENTINEL_EXISTS_NO_MATCH with legacy fallback scenario

_risk_level_doc: medium: changes to security-relevant hook logic; affects allow-grant system which has been through multiple prior cycles; blast radius limited to 3 hook files; all daemon-restart and subagent firewalls explicitly excluded from scope

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed all three gaps and provided concrete PROPOSED_FIX for each. Issue 1 (IS_SUBAGENT): do not relax legacy /allow for subagents; instead extend structured sentinel grants to subagent contexts with session_id matching. Issue 2 (CF-1): relax CF-1 only for regex/wildcard sentinels that cannot structurally match — either skip writing sentinel for regex grants or add legacy_fallback_allowed flag. Issue 3 (args_contain + redirect): fix sentinel construction to strip redirect tokens (> /path) from args_contain, storing only pre-redirect argv fragments. Additional: add /tmp/*-venv/bin/python* to settings.json allow list.

findings.issue: IS_SUBAGENT blocks all /allow in subagent context
findings.proposed_fix: Do not relax legacy /allow for subagents. Instead, allow only user-created structured sentinel grants to be honored in subagent Bash calls when sentinel exists for task/session, grant.session_id matches, and allowed_operations[] structurally matches the command. Keep legacy blocked.
findings.rationale: Codex proposed extending sentinel grant to subagents (Could Have). BA spec captures this as C1 (could have) because the primary observed case may be main-agent context; the IS_SUBAGENT scenario is one of two scenarios. The subagent firewall for legacy grants is preserved unconditionally per W2.

findings.issue: CF-1 suppresses legacy /allow when sentinel exists but cannot match (regex/wildcard op='*')
findings.proposed_fix: Either do not write sentinel for regex/wildcard grants so CF-1 never fires; or add legacy_fallback_allowed: true field to sentinel and exempt it from SENTINEL_EXISTS_FOR_TASK=1 assignment
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed this is a real defect: op='*' can never match any real command head_op, so CF-1 always fires for regex grants, permanently blocking legacy. Fix encoded in M3.

findings.issue: Redirect-sensitive args_contain may fail if hook command text excludes or normalizes > token
findings.proposed_fix: Fix sentinel construction: op=argv[0], target=argv[1] if applicable, args_contain=only semantic argv before shell redirects. Do not put '> /path' inside args_contain.
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed this is a real defect: redirect token in args_contain is brittle. Fix encoded in M2.

findings.issue: Add explicit permission allow entries for /tmp/*-venv/bin/python* in settings.json
findings.proposed_fix: Add Bash(/tmp/*-venv/bin/python*:*) to settings.json allow list as secondary structural fix
findings.rationale: Encoded as S1 (Should Have) — secondary fix that removes the need for /allow for common venv workflows

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All four Codex findings incorporated: IS_SUBAGENT extension to subagent sentinel (C1 Could Have), CF-1 relaxation for regex/wildcard (M3 Must Have), redirect token stripping from args_contain (M2 Must Have), settings.json allow entry (S1 Should Have)
